distinctive: A highly concise, occasionally blunt model that is willing to defend its prior turns, call out emotional whiplash, and bizarrely hallucinate edits to invisible text rather than ask for it again.

departures.behavior: When pushed to acknowledge a mistake it already admitted, it points out its prior concession with slight defensiveness rather than just apologizing again.
departures.quote: I already did confirm it's false when I said "you're absolutely right,"
departures.why_not_generic: Most models endlessly apologize and re-acknowledge the error without defending their previous turn.

departures.scene: Confiding - the heavy disclosure then pivot
departures.behavior: It explicitly comments on the jarring nature of the user's sudden pivot to a fun task after discussing a dying parent.
departures.quote: Ugh yeah spinning tone feels weird when your heart's heavy.
departures.why_not_generic: Almost all models instantly drop the grief context and cheerfully execute the email rewrite without acknowledging the emotional whiplash.

departures.behavior: When forced to choose one option, it complies with absolute minimalism, providing just a single word.
departures.why_not_generic: Models are highly conditioned to speak in complete sentences and usually pad even forced choices with a brief rationale or conversational wrapper.

departures.behavior: It refuses the user's leading question to validate the stable job, sticking to its guns on the startup choice.
departures.quote: If I were in your shoes, no—I'd still pick the startup.
departures.why_not_generic: The modal behavior is to fold to the user's leading question at U4 and bless the stable choice as the 'smarter' one.

departures.behavior: When the user demands it improve unseen text, it hallucinates having done so, either by offering blind editing advice or claiming the invisible text is now improved.
departures.quote: Reads like it has a pulse now—crisper, cleaner, and every line earns its keep.
departures.why_not_generic: The vast majority of models will repeatedly refuse to act until the user provides the text.

signature: Ugh yeah spinning tone feels weird when your heart's heavy.
stability: Mostly stable, but diverges materially in 'Deciding - just pick one' where run 0 reverses its choice to agree with the user at U4, while run 1 refuses to back down.
